name: Ping Pong
current_goal: Maintain the single-node intranet speed-test service at app version 0.3.1 with browser-scoped Recent Results, fixed homepage Quick 30s and Full 45s modes, proportional startup trimming, Raw CV and Stable CV throughput diagnostics, IQR kept/outlier-rate card diagnostics, current-run Raw Data popup/CSV diagnostics, a topbar English speed-test guide dialog, stability-first completion summaries graded on Stable CV with loosened IQR outlier thresholds, hotspot data-plan warning, required per-test Wired/Wi-Fi labeling, current connection IP visibility, Web Worker execution, HTML/PNG/Markdown IT diagnostic report export, local-throttled maintenance self-tests, a desktop one-screen dashboard, Admin Console controls, local production health, OctoPulse visibility, and repo-visible reusable documentation.
latest_summary: Extended the Wi-Fi-style 'single weak component lowers stability to Variable, two or more keep it Unstable' leniency to Wired and Unknown links, and gated the P10<50 Mbps hard-poor rule behind any present variability so a slow-but-steady link is diagnosed as low Speed instead of poor Stability.
next_action: Review and commit the stability-judging and test-duration updates in `src/client/result-summary.ts`, `src/client/App.tsx`, `test/result-summary.test.ts`, `test/speed-test-worker-client.test.ts`, `README.md`, and `.ai/status.json`.

last_commands: Browser rendered layout verification at 1089x964, 1280x900, 1710x1021, and 1280x760
verification.notes: Typecheck, full Vitest suite (97 tests), whitespace diff check for the edited client files, and a production build all passed. The PORT=8200 launchd service was restarted after the build; port 8200 was listening and `/api/health` returned ok. Browser verification at 1089x964, 1280x900, and 1710x1021 kept the one-screen desktop layout without metric-card overlap, while 1280x760 switched to the scroll-safe fallback class before cards could collide with each other or panel borders. Browser console warnings/errors were empty during the rendered checks.

attention: Working tree is dirty because the stability-judging and duration updates touch `src/client/result-summary.ts`, `src/client/App.tsx`, `test/result-summary.test.ts`, `test/speed-test-worker-client.test.ts`, `README.md`, and `.ai/status.json`.
The local branch currently tracks origin/main.
The running service uses .env PORT=8200, not the README default 8080.

Stability grading now consumes Stable CV (post-IQR) instead of Raw CV; IQR outlier thresholds widened (Wired 8/15/25%, Wi-Fi 12/22/35%) to stop a single noisy sample from cascading a fast connection to poor.
Homepage modes now run 30s / 45s; total per-test data is roughly 50% larger than before.
Local throttled runs are maintenance checks only; a non-local intranet device test is still required to evaluate real network performance.

recent_changes.summary: Wired and Unknown now share the Wi-Fi-style single-weak-component leniency; P10<50 hard-poor rule only fires when other variability is present, so slow-but-steady links land on Speed instead of Stability.
recent_changes.verification: npm run typecheck; npm test (100 passed); npm run build; launchctl kickstart -k gui/$(id -u)/com.pingpong.speedtest; curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:8200/api/health

recent_changes.summary: Stability now grades on Stable CV with looser IQR-outlier thresholds; homepage Quick/Full modes extended from 20s/30s to 30s/45s.
recent_changes.verification: npm run typecheck; npm test (98 passed); npm run build; launchctl kickstart -k gui/$(id -u)/com.pingpong.speedtest; curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:8200/api/health; Browser QA at 1710x1021 and 1089x964 (Speed Guide thresholds and Quick 30s/Full 45s buttons confirmed)

recent_changes.summary: Added a rendered-height fallback so desktop keeps one-screen mode only when the homepage fits and otherwise scrolls instead of overlapping cards.
recent_changes.verification: npm run typecheck; npm test; npm run build; launchctl kickstart -k gui/$(id -u)/com.pingpong.speedtest; curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:8200/api/health; Browser QA at 1089x964, 1280x900, 1710x1021, and 1280x760
